commands.ls: List directory contents. Common flags: -l (long format), -a (include hidden), -h (human readable sizes), -R (recursive), -t (sort by time).
commands.cd: Change directory. cd ~ (home), cd / (root), cd .. (parent), cd - (previous). Uses $CDPATH for search.
commands.pwd: Print working directory. Shows absolute path of current directory.
commands.mkdir: Create directory. -p creates parent dirs as needed. Single command: mkdir -p a/b/c creates three nested directories.
commands.rm: Remove files/directories. -r (recursive), -f (force, no confirm). WARNING: rm -rf / destroys system. Always double-check path.
commands.cp: Copy files. cp src dest. -r for directories, -a (archive, preserve attributes), -i (interactive, confirm overwrite).
commands.mv: Move/rename files. mv old new. Works across filesystems (copy+delete). Can move multiple files into a directory.
commands.find: Search for files. find /path -name '*.txt' -type f. Powerful with -exec: find . -name '*.tmp' -exec rm {} \;
commands.grep: Search text with regex. grep -r 'pattern' /path (recursive), -i (case insensitive), -n (line numbers), -l (filenames only).
commands.chmod: Change permissions. Octal: chmod 755 file (rwxr-xr-x). Symbolic: chmod u+x file (add execute for user).
commands.chown: Change owner. chown user:group file. -R for recursive. Only root can change owner, users can change group of their files.
commands.ps: Process status. ps aux (all processes), ps -ef (standard format), top/htop for interactive monitoring.
commands.kill: Send signal to process. kill -15 (SIGTERM, graceful), kill -9 (SIGKILL, force), kill -2 (SIGINT, like Ctrl+C).
commands.tar: Archive utility. tar czf archive.tar.gz /path (create gzip), tar xzf archive.tar.gz (extract), tar tf archive.tar.gz (list).
commands.ssh: Secure Shell. ssh user@host, ssh -p 2222 user@host (custom port), ssh-keygen for key pairs, ssh-copy-id for key installation.
commands.scp: Secure copy over SSH. scp file user@host:/path/. -r for directories. Uses same authentication as ssh.
commands.rsync: Efficient file sync/copy over SSH. rsync -avz src/ user@host:/dest/. Only transfers differences. -a preserves attributes.
commands.curl: HTTP client. curl https://api.example.com, curl -X POST -d 'data' URL, curl -o file URL (save output). Useful for testing APIs.
commands.wget: Download utility. wget URL, wget -r --no-parent URL (recursive). More limited than curl but better for simple downloads.
commands.sed: Stream editor for text transformation. sed 's/old/new/g' file (replace all), sed -n '/pattern/p' file (print matching lines).
commands.awk: Text processing language. awk '{print $1}' file (first column), awk '/pattern/' file (filter). Great for columnar data.
commands.sort: Sort lines. sort -n (numeric), -r (reverse), -k2 (column 2), -u (unique). Often piped with uniq.
commands.uniq: Remove duplicate adjacent lines. Usually used after sort: sort file | uniq -c (count occurrences).
commands.head/tail: Show first/last lines of file. head -n 20 file, tail -f file (follow, for logs), tail -n 100 file.
commands.diff: Compare files line by line. diff file1 file2. Unified format: diff -u file1 file2. Useful for patches.
commands.ln: Create links. ln -s target link (symbolic, like shortcut). ln target link (hard link, same inode).

concepts.File Permissions: Three triples: user (owner), group, others. Each: r (read=4), w (write=2), x (execute=1). Sticky bit (chmod +t): only owner can delete in shared dir. SUID (chmod u+s): runs as file owner.
concepts.Filesystem Hierarchy: / (root), /bin (essential binaries), /etc (config files), /home (user dirs), /var (variable data: logs, spools), /tmp (temporary), /usr (user programs), /opt (optional packages).
concepts.Pipes and Redirection: | (pipe: stdout of one to stdin of next), > (redirect stdout, overwrite), >> (stdout, append), 2> (stderr), &> (both), < (read from file).
concepts.Process Management: Each process has PID, parent PPID. States: running, sleeping, zombie (terminated, waiting for parent to reap). nice/renice for priority. ulimit for resource limits.
concepts.Environment Variables: Set: export VAR=value. Common: PATH (executable search path), HOME, USER, SHELL, LANG, EDITOR. env command lists all. Inherited by child processes.
concepts.Shell Scripting: #!/bin/bash shebang, $1 $@ for arguments, $? for exit code, && and || for conditional execution. set -e (exit on error), set -x (debug mode).
concepts.Package Management: Debian/Ubuntu: apt (apt-get, apt-cache). Red Hat/Fedora: dnf (yum). Arch: pacman. Snap/Flatpak for sandboxed apps. Compile from source with ./configure && make && make install.
concepts.Boot Process: BIOS/UEFI → Bootloader (GRUB) → Kernel init → initramfs → systemd init → target. systemd-analyze blame shows boot time. dmesg shows kernel messages.
concepts.Kernel Modules: Loadable drivers. lsmod (list), modprobe (load with deps), modinfo (details), rmmod (remove). Config in /etc/modprobe.d/. Built-in modules compiled into kernel.
concepts.Systemd: Init system and service manager. systemctl start/stop/restart/enable/disable service. journalctl for logs. Unit files in /etc/systemd/system/. Targets (runlevels): multi-user.target, graphical.target.
concepts.Cron: Schedule recurring tasks. Format: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week command. crontab -e edits user's crontab. /etc/crontab for system. @reboot runs at boot.
concepts.Logging: System logs: /var/log/syslog (or journalctl). auth.log (login attempts), kern.log (kernel), dmesg (boot messages). logrotate manages log files (rotation, compression, deletion).
concepts.Networking: ip addr (show interfaces), ip route (routing table), ss (socket stats, replaces netstat), ping (ICMP test), traceroute (path discovery), iptables/nftables (firewall).
concepts.SSH Keys: Ed25519 recommended: ssh-keygen -t ed25519. Public key in ~/.ssh/authorized_keys. Private key never shared. ssh-agent for key caching. Config in ~/.ssh/config for host aliases.
concepts.Git: See knowledge/git.json for detailed Git knowledge.

troubleshooting.Permission denied: Check file permissions (ls -l), file ownership (ls -n), and whether you're the right user (whoami). Use sudo if you need privileges but don't sudo everything.
troubleshooting.Command not found: Executable not in PATH. Check: which command, or install the package. May need to run hash -r to refresh shell's command cache.
troubleshooting.Disk full: Check: df -h (space per filesystem), du -sh /* (largest dirs at root), lsof | grep deleted (open deleted files holding space). Clean apt cache, old logs, temp files.
troubleshooting.Out of memory: Check: free -h (RAM usage), top (processes sorted by mem), dmesg | grep -i oom (OOM killer). Fix: add swap, limit app memory, close unused programs.
troubleshooting.Process stuck: Check with ps aux, kill with SIGTERM first (-15), escalate to SIGKILL (-9) if needed. Use top -u username to find user's processes. pkill -f pattern kills by name pattern.
troubleshooting.Network unreachable: Check: ip a (interface status), ping gateway, ping 8.8.8.8 (external). Check DNS: nslookup example.com. Check routing: ip route. Check firewall: sudo iptables -L.
troubleshooting.SSH connection refused: Is sshd running? systemctl status sshd. Check port 22 is open: ss -tlnp | grep :22. Check firewall allows SSH: sudo ufw status. Check /etc/ssh/sshd_config for AllowUsers.
troubleshooting.Package install fails: Try: sudo apt update first. Check for held packages: apt-mark showhold. dpkg --configure -a fixes interrupted installs. apt --fix-broken install for dependency issues.
troubleshooting.Service won't start: Check: systemctl status service (shows error), journalctl -xe (recent logs), journalctl -u service (that service's logs). Often a misconfiguration in /etc/ or a port conflict.
troubleshooting.File not found: Check path carefully. Use tab completion. Try find / -name 'filename' 2>/dev/null. Could be a broken symlink: ls -la shows -> target. Use readlink -f for full path.

distros.Ubuntu: Most popular desktop/server distro. Based on Debian. apt package manager. LTS releases every 2 years (current: 24.04). Great community support and documentation.
distros.Debian: Stable, universal OS. Parent of Ubuntu. Three branches: stable (production), testing, unstable (sid). .deb packages with apt. Known for stability over bleeding-edge.
distros.Fedora: Cutting-edge, sponsored by Red Hat. dnf package manager. New kernels and software early. Upgrades every 6 months. Good developer workstation.
distros.Arch Linux: Rolling release (install once, update forever). pacman package manager. AUR (user repository) for community packages. Minimal install, you build up. Requires more manual work.
distros.Alpine Linux: Tiny (~5MB), security-focused. Uses musl libc and busybox. apk package manager. Popular for Docker images and embedded systems.
distros.NixOS: Reproducible, declarative OS. System config in single /etc/nixos/configuration.nix. Nix package manager with purely functional model. Atomic upgrades and rollbacks.
distros.Kali Linux: Penetration testing distro. Based on Debian. Pre-installed with 600+ security tools. Not for daily driving (runs as root by default).
distros.Raspberry Pi OS: Optimized for Raspberry Pi hardware. Based on Debian. Available as Lite (no desktop) or Desktop. Pre-installed on most Pi SD cards.
